meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Master an advanced client-focused workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure your student projects reach production.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects isn't more code; it's a rigid, client-based workflow that treats your personal projects like billable contracts. By implementing strict scoping with Antigravity and automated execution via Claude Code, you force accountability, eliminate feature creep, and ship functional MVPs every single time.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions. You start a project with high energy, only to hit the 'feature creep' wall—that paralyzing moment where the scope expands, the bugs multiply, and the motivation to finish evaporates. It is the silent killer of every student's portfolio.

But professional developers don't rely on motivation; they rely on systems. By reframing your student project as a high-stakes client delivery, you inject the necessary pressure to stay on track. This guide reveals how to use Antigravity for architectural scoping and Claude Code for rapid execution, transforming your development process from a chaotic hobby into a disciplined, shippable product.

steps.title: Define the Contractual Scope
steps.description: Treat your project as a client deliverable. Define exactly what 'finished' looks like to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a strictly scoped project specification document for a SaaS MVP. Focus on core functionality only, excluding all 'nice-to-have' features. Generate a 2-week development milestone calendar.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a roadmap.md file based on the spec provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use AI to build a robust foundation before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable folder structure for a Next.js application using TypeScript. Include configurations for Prisma, Tailwind, and Auth.js.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Create the directory structure and initialize the project based on the Antigravity architecture schema.'

steps.title: Automated Logic Implementation
steps.description: Focus on high-value features first using Claude Code to handle boilerplate and repetitive tasks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a database schema for user authentication and core data storage. Ensure relations are optimized for Prisma.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npx prisma init && npx prisma migrate dev --name init'

steps.title: Iterative Testing Loops
steps.description: Implement a 'test-first' approach to ensure the client (you) stays satisfied with the quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the core authentication routes. Ensure edge cases like invalid emails and duplicate users are covered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run all tests and explain any failures in the current codebase.'

steps.title: Deployment and Sign-off
steps.description: Automate the final push to production to finalize the project milestone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vercel deployment configuration file that includes build commands and environment variable mapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Prepare the project for production deployment and verify all environment variables are set.'

pro_tips: Never write code that isn't directly tied to a milestone identified in your Antigravity spec.
Use Claude Code to document your progress daily; treat it as your 'Client Status Report'.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement, break it into three sub-tasks using Antigravity.
Always commit your code after every successful Claude Code instruction to maintain a clean Git history.
Limit your project to one core 'wow' feature; everything else is secondary to the MVP launch.
resources: Git Strategy for Solo Devs: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your project is deployed, capture your workflow in a README.md, document the lessons learned, and prepare to present it in your next interview.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Stop letting perfectionism hold your career back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
